history_of_present_illness.definition: A breast lift - reshaping and raising the breast tissue and repositioning the nipple higher.
Overly large breasts, which can cause neck, shoulder, and back pain.
The nipple and the coloured skin around it, kept on its own blood supply and moved higher during a lift.

physical_exam.definition: The scar shape from a breast reduction - around the areola, straight down, and along the breast fold.
Means blood is flowing well through the tissue. A well-perfused nipple is a healthy sign.
A collection of blood under the skin. Sudden severe swelling or pressure at a wound can signal this and needs urgent review.

assessment.definition: When a wound edge starts to open or separate. This is one of the signs to report to the practice.

plan.definition: An antibiotic given for a short course after surgery to help prevent wound infection.
A regular pain reliever (Panadol). Taking it around the clock keeps pain steady and reduces the need for stronger medicine.
